Tuticorin: after people were killed after police opened fire protesters marching against expansion Sterlite Copper Tamil Nadu's Tuticorin, another protester lost life fresh police firing Wednesday.
Kaliappan, 23-year-old, shot when group youths were pelting police vehicle with stones.
protest, which been over days now, estimated 15,000 people march towards collectorate Tuesday.
Residents area have been seeking immediate closure Sterlite unit since February this year.
They allege waste from unit contaminated water bodies region, that they facing severe health problems.
industrial unit centre controversy country's biggest copper smelters, which owned Vedanta majority-owned subsidiary London-listed Vedanta Resources.
company seeking double capacity smelter 800,000 tonnes year.
Operations plant been stopped state government earlier were allowed resume National Green Tribunal.
Section remains imposed Tuticorin.
Many commercial establishments city remain closed.
Central Forces have also been kept standby provide assistance required.
Security tightened outside Tuticorin's General Hospital, where injured undergoing treatment.
Protesters also ablaze.
Retired judge Aruna Jagadeesan been appointed Tamil Nadu government probe police firing Tuticorin.
state government also announced judicial inquiry.
Madurai bench Madras High Court stayed expansion Sterlite plant Wednesday.
bench insisted that company consult with public before expanding unit.
Shares Vedanta fell their lowest since July 2017 after protests.
Congress president Rahul Gandhi claimed protesters were killed rejecting ideology Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh.
Several celebrities Tamil Nadu have condemned incident, including Kamal Haasan Rajinikanth.
Kamal Haasan visited injured Thoothukudi while Rajinikanth tweeted video condemning incident.
